Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic identified liquid damage on the logic board. We will need to complete our MacBook liquid damage treatment to properly clean, stabilise, and restore all the affected components. * Charging port: We also found clear liquid damage on the charging port, as shown in the image attached. Because the port has been affected by liquid ingress, it is no longer working and will need to be replaced to restore stable charging. * Battery: The battery has become faulty as a direct result of the liquid damage. The charging port and battery are connected through the same power distribution and charging circuits. When liquid entered this area, it damaged the Battery Management System (BMS), which is a small control board built into the battery itself. The BMS is responsible for allowing the battery to communicate with the logic board and report battery data correctly. Since this component is no longer functioning, the system cannot read the battery, and the battery will need to be replaced to restore normal operation.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, please see your options below.  Required Repairs ----------------  * MacBook Pro 13 M2 A2338 Battery: £199 * MacBook Pro 13 M2 A2338 Charging Port: £159 * MacBook Liquid Damage Treatment: £149 Total: £507 inc VAT discounted to £429 (save £78)  Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 3 working days once approved.

Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic confirmed extensive liquid damage across the logic board, affecting multiple critical power and data circuits. This level of damage explains why the MacBook is not powering on. To restore stable operation, the affected areas will require full board-level rebuilding and thorough cleaning to remove all residue. * Charging ports: Liquid damage has affected both charging ports. Both will need to be replaced. * Touch ID: Touch ID is not working due to liquid damage affecting the sensor line. To fix the issue, we would need to order the replacement Touch ID from Apple, but this would arrive after the holidays. * Data status: The data is safe and accessible. If preferred, we can proceed with a data-only repair focused solely on data recovery rather than full functional restoration.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, and your data is safe. Please see your options below.  Required Repairs ----------------  * MacBook Pro 14 M1 Pro/Max A2442 Tier 3 Logic Board Repair & Liquid Damage Treatment: £399 * MacBook Pro 14 M1 Pro/Max A2442 Charging Ports: £199 * MacBook Pro 14 M1 Pro/Max A2442 Touch ID: £199 Total: £797 inc VAT discounted to £648 (save £149)  Repair Details --------------  We can complete all repairs apart from the Touch ID by tomorrow (our last day before the holidays). To repair the Touch ID, we would need to order the required part once we’re back from the holidays on the 5th of January. The part typically arrives within 5 to 7 working days. In the meantime, we would temporarily disconnect the Touch ID from the device. This means the fingerprint sensor and the power button functionality would not work during that period, but the MacBook would remain fully usable otherwise.

Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Liquid ingress has affected the main logic board, impacting multiple critical circuits responsible for power distribution, data handling, and component communication. To restore stable and reliable operation, the logic board would require full replacement or extensive board-level rebuilding. * Screen: Liquid ingress has damaged the display, causing it to fail. The screen would need to be fully replaced to restore normal visual output and touch functionality. * Charging port: Liquid contamination has affected the charging port as well. A full charging port replacement would be required to restore reliable power input. * Rear cameras: Liquid ingress has reached the rear camera module, resulting in the component failure. The camera module would need to be replaced. * Face ID system: Liquid damage is present in the Face ID area, and it caused it to fail. To replace it, we would need to order the part directly from Apple. * Housing and internal areas: Liquid has spread throughout the internal housing, affecting multiple internal areas and increasing the risk of corrosion and long-term instability. The overall housing and internal structure would need addressing as part of any full functional repair.  Due to the extent and spread of the liquid damage across multiple critical components, the only feasible repair would be a data recovery. This would involve repairing the device enough to allow us to complete a full backup of your data to your iCloud account. We can confirm that the device contains approximately 480 GB of data, so a 2 TB iCloud plan would be required to ensure sufficient space (more on this here: https://www.apple.com/uk/icloud/). This process will need to be completed after we return from the holiday break, as it will take several days to carry out safely. Once completed, we will confirm as soon as the backup is finished so you can restore the data onto your replacement device.
@Safan Patel client accepted data recovery on iCloud. Please proceed.

------------ Faults Found ------------  * System / T2 firmware: When the device arrived, it was stuck in a state with no operating system loading. To stabilise the MacBook and proceed with diagnostics, we first restored the T2 security chip. This chip manages core firmware and security features such as Touch ID, Apple Wallet, and system integrity. When its firmware becomes corrupted, it can prevent macOS from loading correctly. Restoring the T2 resolved the software loop and allowed us to continue testing the hardware. * Touch Bar: Once the system was restored, we identified a fault with the Touch Bar. The Touch Bar itself is damaged and the display is no longer functioning. This is a hardware failure, and the Touch Bar will need to be replaced to restore normal operation. * Keyboard: We also found that the keyboard is sticky, which is consistent with liquid exposure. This indicates that liquid has affected the internal keyboard membrane. While the keyboard is currently still working, damage at this level often worsens over time and can lead to further failures. For this reason, we strongly recommend replacing the keyboard now, as any future issues caused by this damage would not be covered under warranty. * Battery: The battery health is currently reading at 80%. This is not a fault, but it does indicate normal wear and tear and that the battery is becoming depleted. Battery replacement is optional at this stage, but we do recommend it to maintain reliable performance and avoid unexpected shutdowns in the near future.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * Touch Bar: £249 Total: £249 inc VAT  ---------------- Optional Repairs ----------------  * Battery: £199 * Keyboard: £249 Total (including battery): £448 inc VAT discounted to £399 (save £49)  Total (including keyboard): £498 inc VAT discounted to £429 (save £69)  Total (including both): £697 inc VAT discounted to £549 (save £148)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 2-3 working days once approved and paid.

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic confirmed severe damage on the logic board, primarily caused by liquid ingress. Despite what was previously advised by the other repair shop, the liquid damage on the board has not been fully repaired and is still present. The most critical issue we identified is around the camera connector on the logic board. This connector is responsible for transferring signal data between the MacBook’s camera and the logic board. In this case, liquid damage and an electrical short in this area have caused the connector to fail.  In addition to the connector itself, the combination of liquid contamination and the resulting short has affected surrounding circuits and chips. These areas will need to be fully rebuilt at board level to ensure long-term reliability and prevent repeat failure. The camera connector will need to be replaced, and the damaged area beneath it will need to be properly treated and rebuilt. * Display: The camera connector runs between the logic board and the display assembly. Because the short originated on the logic board and travelled through the camera connection, the display-side circuitry has also been damaged. This has caused the display to fail completely. As a result, a full screen repair will be required in addition to the logic board work. We can confirm that the device is fully repairable. Your data has not been affected, and once the required repairs are completed, you will be able to continue using the MacBook normally.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * Tier 3 Logic Board Repair: £399 * Display Repair: £649 Total: £1,048 inc VAT discounted to £879 (save £169)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 2 working days once approved and paid.

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic identified extensive liquid damage on the logic board. The liquid ingress has affected multiple circuits, in particular the screen-related circuitry, including the backlight circuit and the image output circuit. This damage is the root cause of several of the symptoms observed on the device. * Screen: We confirmed that the liquid damage affecting the logic board caused a chain reaction that also damaged the display. The screen is electrically linked to the logic board through several circuits, including the backlight and image output circuits mentioned above. As a result of the failure on these lines, the circuitry within the screen itself has failed. Unfortunately, this type of damage is not repairable at component level and the display will need to be replaced. * Keyboard: We also identified an issue with the keyboard, where the key input feedback is inconsistent. This is caused by liquid ingress affecting the internal key network beneath the keyboard. Due to the same liquid damage, the keyboard backlight has also been affected and is no longer functioning correctly. Both the keyboard and the keyboard backlight will require replacement to restore proper operation.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, and your data is safe. Trackpad fault

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Trackpad: The trackpad is not working correctly due to liquid damage. During inspection, we found signs of liquid ingress affecting the trackpad circuitry, which is why the issue is intermittent and cannot be resolved with cleaning alone. The trackpad will need to be replaced. * Battery: We also identified an issue with the battery, which is not being correctly read by the logic board. When this happens, it means the battery’s BMS (Battery Management System), which is a small logic circuit built into the battery itself, has failed. Because the BMS can no longer communicate properly with the logic board, the system cannot read battery data, making the battery effectively unusable. In this case, the battery will need to be replaced.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * MacBook Air 13 M2 A2681 Diagnostic: £49 * MacBook Air 13 M2 A2681 Trackpad: £249 * MacBook Air 13 M2 A2681 Battery: £249 Total: £547 inc VAT discounted to £429 (save £118)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 3 working days once approved.

The device is displaying a high-temperature warning because liquid has affected the logic board and caused the temperature sensor circuit to short. The liquid damage is located between the internal layers of the logic board, which means the board will need to be split, the layers cleaned thoroughly, and any damaged circuits repaired. * Charging port: The charging port is not working. We did not find direct evidence of liquid damage on the port itself, however it is likely that liquid damage on the logic board has affected the charging port flex line, leading to failure. * Battery: The battery is currently at 86% health. This is not considered faulty and is still operating normally. A genuine battery replacement would be optional. * Screen: We identified that the screen has been previously replaced with a non-original component. The display is currently working so replacement is not required at this stage, especially considering the cost (£339). ------------ Faults Found ------------  * Trackpad: Our diagnostic confirmed that the fault is internal to the trackpad assembly. While the visible signs of liquid exposure around the edges were minimal, further inspection under the microscope revealed corrosion on the solder pads of the trackpad controller chip. This corrosion has compromised the controller’s ability to communicate correctly with the logic board, which explains the intermittent failure. The trackpad will require replacement to restore proper functionality. * MagSafe Charging Port: We also identified burn marks on the contact pins of the MagSafe charging port. Although the port is still functioning at the moment, heat damage to the pins increases the risk of intermittent charging or future failure. Replacement is recommended to prevent potential charging instability or further electrical damage. We can confirm that your MacBook Air 13 M2 A2681 is repairable. This circuit is responsible for distributing stable power from the battery to the other components during startup and normal operation. The fault was preventing proper power flow across the board, resulting in a complete no-power condition. The affected power circuitry will need to be rebuilt to restore stable and reliable operation. * Battery: We also identified that the battery is not detected by the device. The battery contains an internal management system that regulates charging, monitors health, and communicates with the logic board to ensure safe power delivery. When this system degrades or fails, the device may not detect it any longer. The battery will require replacement to ensure stable performance once the board-level fault is addressed. ------------ Faults Found ------------  Our diagnostic confirmed liquid damage affecting the main power distribution, power management, and charging circuits on the logic board. These circuits are responsible for regulating incoming power, distributing stable voltage throughout the device, and allowing the phone to initialise correctly during startup. Due to corrosion within these areas, the board is not able to deliver consistent power, which is why the device will not power on.  The affected circuits can be repaired to restore stable power temporarily. This will allow the device to boot and remain operational long enough to complete a full data backup. However, the overall extent of liquid damage means the phone cannot be restored to reliable long-term use.  The purpose of the repair would therefore be data recovery only. Once powered and stabilised, we can complete a backup to your iCloud account, provided sufficient storage is available, or transferring the data to another device.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * Tier 3 Logic Board Repair (Data Recovery): £299 Total: £299 inc VAT  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 2-5 working days once approved.

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic identified a logic board level fault affecting the main power distribution line. This line is part of the MacBook’s primary power delivery system and is responsible for distributing regulated power to multiple subsystems during startup and normal operation. This rail is critical for initial power sequencing, and when it's compromised the system cannot correctly initialise its power stages, resulting in a no-power condition. We can confirm that this is a repairable logic board fault, and the affected power circuit will need to be rebuilt to restore stable power delivery and allow the device to power on normally. * Keyboard (optional): We also identified signs of liquid exposure affecting the keyboard assembly. The keyboard is currently functional, however, liquid residue within the keyboard layers can lead to corrosion over time and may cause intermittent or complete key failure in the future. For this reason, a keyboard replacement is recommended as a preventative repair to avoid potential long-term issues, though it is not required to restore the device to a full working condition.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, and your data is safe. Please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * Tier 3 Logic Board Repair: £349 Total: £349 inc VAT  ---------------- Optional Repairs ----------------  * Keyboard Replacement: £249 Total: £598 inc VAT discounted to £499 (save £99)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 2 working days once approved.

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic identified a fault on the always-on power line monitoring path. This circuit is part of the device’s always-on power system and allows the power management side of the logic board to measure and control how power is being delivered at startup. When this line is compromised, the MacBook can fail to power on or behave inconsistently because the system cannot correctly initialise and regulate power. We can confirm this is repairable, and the affected circuit will need to be fully rebuilt to restore normal power-up and stability. * Battery: We also found that the battery has been affected as a direct result of this power fault. On these models, the battery management system (BMS) relies on stable always-on power and correct power monitoring to communicate consistently with the logic board. The fault on the logic board caused instability on the always-on power line, which in turn overloaded the BMS. As a result, the battery is no longer able to communicate reliably with the logic board and will require replacement. We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, and your data is safe. Please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * Tier 2 Logic Board Repair: £299 * Battery: £249 Total: £548 inc VAT discounted to £499 (save £49)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 2 working days once approved.

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Screen: Our diagnostic confirmed that the LCD panel has been affected by liquid damage, causing it to not display any image. To further confirm our findings, we tested the lid with a known working LCD panel, and the screen backlight still showed signs of liquid damage, confirming that the issue originates from liquid ingress to the display circuitry. The LCD panel will need to be repaired to restore display functionality, and the backlight panels replaced to remove the liquid stains.  * Keyboard and Trackpad: Our diagnostic confirmed that the keyboard and its backlight have been affected by liquid damage. This has caused both the keyboard and trackpad to stop working. The trackpad shares the same circuit line as the keyboard, so when one is damaged, the other is often affected as well. The good news is that we don't need to replace the trackpad separately, as replacing the keyboard will restore both functions.  * Hall Sensor: The hall sensor controls the sleep and wake function of the display. Our diagnostic confirmed that this sensor has suffered liquid damage and is no longer communicating correctly. This means the screen doesn't know when to go to sleep. The hall sensor will need to be repaired to restore proper functionality.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * MacBook Air 13 M4 A3240 Screen & Backlight: £499 * MacBook Air 13 M4 A3240 Keyboard & Backlight: £299 * MacBook Air 13 M4 A3240 Hall Sensor: £149 Total: £947 inc VAT discounted to £699 (save £248)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 3 working days once approved.

Their reply:    Hello Support!Reina here from the Dublin technical support team🍀 - to help you tackle your query! I'll be taking over here for now! :)  So, when you mentioned creating tickets via API and Fin's responses only showing in the inbox, that's actually the expected behavior unless you also tell Intercom to treat it as an email conversation. By default, API-created tickets are treated as Messenger conversations, which is why Fin doesn't send emails for them. The fix should be straightforward then! :)  * For tickets: add "channel": "email" to your API request body * For conversations: set "message_type": "email" Once that's in place, to my knowledge at least, Fin will recognize it as an email conversation and send responses directly to the contact's email address. This should let you skip the 30-45 second delay and participant swap entirely. But! just one thing to note, the channel parameter for tickets requires API version 2.12 or later. If you're on an older version, you'll need to update your API version header, or you could use the conversations endpoint with message_type instead. Let me know how you get on with this, or if anything's unclear. :)

records.item_name: Map out the In-Repair Status Updates + Fin AI Integration
records.text: :clipboard: Repair Status Sync — Monday → Intercom → Finn  Objective: Enable Finn to auto-respond to customers chasing repair updates via a single repair_stage Intercom contact attribute synced from Monday.  Architecture: Monday status change (webhook) → Cloudflare Worker (conditional logic) → Intercom API (update contact attribute) → Finn checks attribute when customer messages  Step 1: Create "Under QC" Status on Monday Board: Main Board (349212843), Column: status4 Brief Ronnie: use "Under QC" instead of "Diagnostics" for QC.  Step 2: Create Intercom Contact Attribute Name: repair_stage | Type: String | Level: Contact  Step 3: Status Mapping (priority order, first match wins)  | # | Condition | Value | | --- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- | | 1 | Status = Queued For Repair | `queued_for_repair` | | 2 | Status = Diagnostics, Repair Type = Diagnostic, Diag column empty | `in_diagnostic` | | 3 | Status = Repair Paused, Repair Type = Diagnostic, Diag column empty | `diagnostic_paused` | | 4 | Status = Diagnostic Complete | `awaiting_quote` | | 5 | Status = Under Repair/Under Refurb + (Diag column filled OR Repair Type = Repair) | `under_repair` | | 6 | Status = Repaired + Repair column filled | `awaiting_qc` | | 7 | Status = Under QC (new) | `awaiting_qc` | | 8 | Status = Password Req + Passcode empty | `awaiting_password` | | 9 | Status = Password Req + Passcode filled | `password_incorrect` | | 10 | Status = Awaiting Part | `awaiting_part` | | 11 | Status = Client To Contact | `client_contact_needed` | | 12 | Status = Book Courier + Received date empty | `awaiting_dispatch` | | 13 | Status = Book Return Courier + Received date filled | `awaiting_dispatch` | | 14 | Status = Ready To Collect | `ready_to_collect` | | 15 | Status = Received | `received` | | 16 | Status = Shipped/Returned | `dispatched` | Monday Column IDs: status4 (Status), status24 (Repair Type), multiple_person_mkwqj321 (Diagnostic), multiple_person_mkwqy930 (Repair), text8 (Passcode), date4 (Received Date)  Step 4: Finn Response Templates  • received → "We've received your device. It's in our intake queue and will be assigned shortly." • queued_for_repair → "Your device is in our repair queue. A technician will pick it up as soon as a workstation is available." • in_diagnostic → "Your device is currently being diagnosed. We'll update you once complete." • diagnostic_paused → "The diagnostic has been paused temporarily. We'll resume shortly." • awaiting_quote → "Diagnostic is complete. We're preparing a quote — you should receive it shortly." • under_repair → "Your device is currently being repaired by one of our specialists." • awaiting_qc → "Repair complete — currently undergoing quality control checks." • awaiting_password → "We need your device passcode to proceed. Could you please share it?" • password_incorrect → "The passcode provided doesn't seem to work. Could you double-check?" • awaiting_part → "We're waiting on a part to arrive. We'll update you when work resumes." [10:42 AM]• client_contact_needed → "Our team needs to discuss something about your repair. Someone will reach out shortly." • awaiting_dispatch → "Your device is ready — we're arranging the courier. Tracking details coming soon." • ready_to_collect → "Your repair is complete and ready for collection! Bring valid photo ID." • dispatched → "Your device has been dispatched and is on its way back to you." • Empty/not set → "Let me check on that — I'll get the team to look into your status." (escalate)  Implementation Checklist: ☐ Create "Under QC" status on Monday ☐ Brief Ronnie on new status ☐ Create repair_stage attribute in Intercom ☐ Build Cloudflare Worker with mapping logic ☐ Set up Monday webhook on status4 changes ☐ Test each mapping end-to-end ☐ Update Finn guidance doc ☐ Monitor first week  Full spec saved to docs. This does NOT affect existing automated emails (n8n workflow TDBSUDxpcW8e56y4).

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: We have completed the diagnostic and confirmed that the device is taking power, but there is no image, no backlight, and no keyboard response. This is due to liquid damage on the logic board. The affected area involves power management and distribution circuits, which are responsible for supplying stable voltage to the display, backlight, and input systems. Because these circuits are unstable, the affected components are not receiving the power required to function correctly. The damaged sections of the board will need to be rebuilt to restore proper power flow and functionality. * Keyboard and keyboard backlight: We also found signs of liquid exposure affecting the keyboard. The keyboard is currently operational, but several keys feel sticky or uneven, which indicates liquid has entered the internal membrane layers. The keyboard backlight has also been exposed to moisture. While both are functioning at present, liquid exposure can cause gradual deterioration and eventual failure. Replacement of the keyboard and backlight assembly is optional at this stage but recommended to prevent future issues.  We can confirm that your MacBook Pro is repairable, please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * Tier 1 Logic Board Repair: £199 * MacBook Liquid Damage Treatment: £99 Total: £298 inc VAT discounted to £249 (save £49)  ---------------- Optional Repairs ----------------  * Keyboard & Keyboard Backlight: £299 Total: £548 inc VAT discounted to £429 (save £168 in total)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 2 working days once approved.

------------ Faults Found ------------  Our diagnostic confirmed that the device has sustained severe liquid damage. Liquid ingress has affected all major internal components, including the display, Face ID, charging port, rear camera modules, logic board, and internal housing. Liquid exposure causes corrosion and electrical shorting across both power and data circuits, which prevents stable operation and leads to multi-system failure. In this case, the contamination is widespread and has compromised multiple functional areas of the device.  Despite the extent of the damage, we have confirmed that the internal storage remains intact and the data is accessible. To recover the data safely, the device will require a controlled partial board-level repair to restore stable power. A further staged repair will then be required to allow the device to remain operational long enough to complete a full iCloud backup. Once the backup is completed, the data can be restored onto a replacement device.  This device, however, is beyond economical repair.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * iPhone Data Recovery Repair: £299 Total: £299 inc VAT  -------------- Repair Details --------------  The data recovery repair will be completed within 2-3 working days once approved.

🚶 WALK-IN CUSTOMER  Created automatically from walk-in Typeform. Intercom ticket: https://app.intercom.com/a/inbox/pt6lwaq6/inbox/conversation/215473122053717  Device: iPad Model: iPad Pro 12.9" (6G) Issue: Charging port Reviewed prices online: No

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Display: Our diagnostic confirmed that the screen backlight itself has been damaged by liquid. The LCD panel shows clear internal staining, which indicates that liquid has entered the display layers. The backlight is what allows you to see the image clearly and evenly across the screen. Once liquid penetrates the panel, it causes permanent marks and uneven brightness that cannot be cleaned or reversed. Because the damage is within the LCD assembly itself, the panel will need to be replaced. * Hall Sensor: We also found liquid damage affecting the lid angle sensor. This component detects when the lid is opened or closed and controls the device’s sleep and wake behaviour. It is also linked to several related systems, including display activation and input response from the keyboard and trackpad. When this sensor is faulty, it can cause the screen to stop displaying properly and may also lead to keyboard and trackpad faults. As corrosion is already present, the affected area will need to be repaired to prevent further faults in the future.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, and your data is safe. Please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * MacBook Pro 13 M4 A3240 Screen: £479 * MacBook Pro 13 M4 A3240 Hall Sensor: £199 Total: £678 inc VAT discounted to £549 (save £129)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  We can complete the screen repair within two working days once approved. With regards to the lid angle sensor, this part must be ordered directly from Apple and typically takes between 5-7 working days to arrive. We can proceed with the screen replacement first and then have the device return once the sensor has arrived, if that works for you. Please note that once the sensor has been delivered to us, you will have 3 working days to bring the device back in. After that point, we would need to return the part.

records.item_name: Write thought processes involved when dealing with enquiries
records.text: @Ricky Panesar we can discuss about this on a call    OpenClaw_Enquiry_Thought_Process.docx  iCorrect_Client_Communication_Audit.docx

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic identified a board-level fault primarily within the charging circuit. As a result, the device is not receiving stable power and is unable to charge properly. In addition, we identified faults within the touch input circuit and part of the main power distribution circuit. These circuits are responsible for registering screen input and delivering regulated voltage across the board. All of these issues are repairable, and they require separate board-level repairs across different sections of the logic board to restore full functionality. * Battery: We also noted that the battery health is currently at 80%. While this is not the cause of the current fault and is not required to complete the repair, the device would benefit from a replacement to improve performance and longevity. If you would like to proceed with this as well, we would install an original Apple battery during the repair.  We can confirm that your iPhone is repairable, and your data is safe. Please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * iPhone 15 Pro Tier 2 Logic Board Repair: £249 Total: £249 inc VAT  ---------------- Optional Repairs ----------------  * iPhone 15 Pro Battery: £99 Total: £348 inc VAT discounted to £319 (save £29)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 1-2 working days once approved.

------------ Faults Found ------------  We have now completed the diagnostic on your iPhone. During our testing we confirmed that the device has previously been worked on at logic board level, particularly around the charging circuitry. The board shows multiple faults and prior repair attempts, which is why the device is currently unable to power on and function normally.  After diagnosing the board, we can confirm that it is possible for us to carry out targeted repairs to stabilise the logic board enough to temporarily power the device on. This would allow us to access the data and complete a full iCloud backup to your Apple ID account. Due to the number of issues present on the logic board, completing a full repair to restore the phone to normal working condition would technically be possible but would not make sense cost-wise. Instead, the most sensible approach is to proceed with a data recovery repair.  This type of repair is quite complex, as we need to carefully stabilise the board enough for the phone to power on and remain stable long enough for us to complete a full iCloud backup without the device failing during the process. Once completed, all your photos, information, and data will be safely stored on your iCloud account.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * iPhone Tier 3 Data Recovery Repair: £399 Total: £399 inc VAT  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 3-5 working days once approved.

Hi Olivia,  I hope you are well. I'm re-sending the quote after our conversation. I've revised it given you already have context.  We have completed our diagnostic on your iPhone 13 and identified the following faults:  ------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic Board: We identified a fault affecting the logic board which is preventing the rear cameras from operating correctly. The circuits responsible for communicating with the camera modules are not functioning as intended, which is why the cameras are currently not recognised by the system. The affected area of the logic board will need to be repaired in order to restore normal camera functionality. * Face ID: We also identified a fault with the TrueDepth camera system, which is responsible for Face ID. This module is not responding, meaning Face ID cannot operate. Due to the way Apple pairs this component to the device, the only available repair option for this issue is a replacement through Apple, which would cost £249. * Charging Port: The charging port is intermittently working and the device is unable to reliably receive power through the connector. The charging port will therefore need to be replaced to restore proper charging functionality. * Vibration Motor: The vibration motor is not working when the device attempts to trigger haptic feedback. This component will need to be replaced to restore vibration functionality. * Battery: The battery currently installed in the device is not an original Apple battery. While it is still functioning, it has been flagged during our assessment as a non-original component. Most of the issues identified are consistent with damage that can occur during a rear glass replacement. If the repair carried out by iSmash involved replacing the rear glass only, the process typically requires the use of a laser machine, heat, and manual scraping tools to remove the broken glass fragments from the frame.  During this process, sensitive components located underneath the rear glass, such as the rear camera modules and the charging port assembly, can be exposed to prolonged heat or mechanical stress. In some cases this can damage the flex connections or surrounding circuits, which may explain the faults we have identified.  We can confirm that your iPhone is repairable, please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * iPhone 13 Tier 1 Logic Board Repair: £149 * iPhone 13 Charging Port: £89 * iPhone 13 Battery: £79 * iPhone 13 Vibration Motor: £0 Total: £317 inc VAT discounted to £268 (save £49)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 1 working day once approved.

We have completed our diagnostic on your A2992 MacBook Pro and identified the following faults:  Logic Board (Wi-Fi Area): Our diagnostic confirmed that there are signs of liquid damage affecting the logic board in the area surrounding the Wi-Fi ICs. This explains the intermittent issue with the Wi-Fi as well as the progressive worsening of the fault over time. To resolve this, the affected area of the logic board will need to be repaired and the liquid damage will need to be treated thoroughly.  Charging Ports: Our diagnostic also confirmed that two charging ports have failed due to liquid damage. For the purpose of this quote, we have included one charging port repair, which would address the immediate issue being quoted for.  Keyboard (Optional): Our diagnostic also confirmed that there are signs of liquid exposure around the keyboard area. The keyboard is still working at the moment, but liquid exposure can cause internal corrosion over time, which means there is a risk it may stop working in the future. If you would like to address this preventatively, the keyboard can be replaced alongside the required repairs. This repair is optional at this stage.  Screen (Optional): Our diagnostic also confirmed that the screen has been affected in the area of the dent on the lid, which has caused a backlight issue. To resolve this properly, the display would need to be dismantled so the LED strips behind the LCD panel can be replaced, and the dent in the rear housing would also need to be pushed back out from inside the lid. Because the housing is metal, the dent can be improved but there will still be visible signs of distress around the affected area. The screen is still usable at the moment, so this repair is optional, but there is a risk that the pressure may spread further into the LCD panel over time and cause additional damage.  We can confirm that your A2992 MacBook Pro is repairable, please see your options below. ------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic Board: Our diagnostic confirmed a failure within the main power circuit of the logic board. This circuit is responsible for distributing power throughout the device and allowing the rest of the circuits to function normally. When this line fails, the device cannot turn on, which is exactly what we’re seeing with your iPad. More specifically, we found that one of the main microchips on this line has suffered a localised explosion. This means it received an incorrect amount of current, which caused it to fail and brought the entire line down with it. The good news is that this can be repaired by rebuilding the affected circuit. * Screen: We also noted minor physical damage to the glass near the bottom of the charging port area. This is purely cosmetic and has not worsened during our assessment. We’ve included it as an optional repair, but it’s not required to get the device working again. We have completed our diagnostic on your iPhone 14 Pro Max and identified the following faults:  Proximity Sensor: The device arrived randomly restarting every few minutes. Upon diagnosing it, we confirmed that the proximity sensor was damaged. A faulty or incorrectly fitted proximity sensor on this model causes the device to restart in exactly this way, as the iPhone is unable to properly communicate with the top microphone and the other parts located on the flex. To resolve this, the proximity sensor flex will need to be repaired. We can also confirm that this repair will not affect Face ID in any way.  Charging Port: After confirming the proximity sensor fault, we also noticed that another component on the charging port was causing further restarts. This was confirmed via the panic logs stored in the device itself. We can fix the issue by replacing the charging port.  Screen (Optional): The screen currently fitted is a non-genuine copy panel. While it is functional, it does not match the original display quality of the device. If you would like to restore the screen to its original specification, we can replace it with a genuine OLED panel at the same time.  Battery (Optional): The battery capacity is currently at 81%. The battery is still functional, so this repair is optional. If you would like to address this at the same time, the battery can be replaced alongside the other repairs.  We can confirm that your iPhone 14 Pro Max is repairable, please see your options below. Hi Chi,  I hope you are well, and thank you for your patience.  We have now completed our diagnostic on your iPad Pro 12.9" and wanted to share our findings with you.  We could see that a previous repair attempt had already been made on the logic board, and that a number of areas had been heavily worked on. Upon diagnosing it, we confirmed faults within the charging circuit, the RAM and the ROM, among other areas on the board.  While testing the rest of the device, we also identified a fault with the screen itself. The display stays black most of the time, and when it does come on, it shows lines. On top of that, Face ID is also damaged and cannot be repaired.  For that reason, we would not be able to offer this as a full repair back to normal use. What we can offer instead is a data recovery repair. This would involve carrying out a partial logic board repair to bring the device on long enough for us to push an iCloud backup to your Apple ID account.  The cost for this would be £349 inc VAT, and it would be completed in around 2 to 3 working days after the Easter break.  You can view the logic board state in the image we have taken during our diagnostic here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1CGTT6cAEZNdUYLWg_7mL4mEfdH6qO5v2/view?usp=drive_link  If you would like to proceed, please let us know and we will take it from there. We have completed our diagnostic on your A2485 MacBook Pro and identified the following faults:  Logic Board: Our diagnostic confirmed that liquid damage has affected multiple areas of the logic board, with the main issue sitting within the audio circuit. This is one of the main circuits on the board, so the fact that only the right side speaker has been affected is actually a positive sign, as more severe damage in this area can stop the device from turning on altogether. To resolve this properly, the affected area of the audio circuit will need to be repaired, and the logic board will also need a thorough liquid damage treatment to remove any trace of contamination from the surface and from underneath the micro components.  Charging Port: Our diagnostic also confirmed that one of the charging ports has been affected by liquid damage. Because of this fault, the charging port will need to be replaced.  Keyboard (Optional): Our diagnostic also confirmed that there are signs of liquid on the keyboard. The keyboard is still working at the moment, but liquid exposure can cause internal corrosion over time, which means there is a risk it may stop working in the future. If you would like to address this preventatively, the keyboard can be replaced alongside the required repairs. This repair is optional at this stage.  We can confirm that your A2485 MacBook Pro is repairable, please see your options below. We have completed our diagnostic on your MacBook Pro 14" (M3 Pro, 2023) and identified the following faults:  Hall Sensor: Our diagnostic confirmed that the hall sensor has been affected by liquid damage. This component is responsible for the sleep/wake functionality of the device, and it tells the MacBook to go to sleep when the lid is closed and wake up when it is opened. It also plays a role in trackpad input. Due to this damage, the device currently has no display output, and the trackpad is unresponsive. To resolve this, the hall sensor will need to be replaced.  Logic Board (Liquid Damage): Our diagnostic also confirmed that liquid damage is present on the logic board. While the board is currently functioning, liquid residue left on a logic board can cause corrosion over time and lead to component failure in the future. To prevent this from progressing, a liquid damage treatment will need to be carried out to clean the affected area.  Charging Ports: Our diagnostic also confirmed that two of the USB-C charging ports have been affected by liquid and have corroded as a result. The corrosion is causing a short circuit on the logic board that is making the device restart. To resolve the issue, the charging ports will need to be replaced.  Keyboard (Optional): Our diagnostic also confirmed that the keyboard has been affected by liquid. The keyboard is still functioning at the moment, but liquid exposure can cause internal corrosion over time, which means there is a risk it may stop working in the future. If you would like to address this at the same time, the keyboard can be replaced alongside the required repairs.  We can confirm that your MacBook Pro 14" is repairable, please see your options below. Original issue:  Device was not turning on. The customer had continued using the MacBook with an external monitor after the screen was damaged. Over time, the keyboard stopped working and the device eventually powered off completely. The device had been opened by another repair shop prior to arriving with us, and no liquid damage had been identified by them. Data was not a priority, as the customer only wanted the device working with an external display.  Diagnostic report:  Upon inspection, we found clear evidence of liquid damage on the logic board, affecting multiple areas including the audio circuit and surrounding regions. Several areas had already been previously worked on. We carried out board-level cleaning and repair attempts on the affected areas, but the device remained non-functional. Further investigation identified damage to a CPU data line resistor, likely caused during a previous repair attempt. After repairing this line, the device was able to power on and function correctly. We also noted that the screen was damaged, and there was evidence of liquid exposure around the keyboard, which was still working at the time but at risk of failing.  Quote:  We quoted for a Tier 3 logic board repair at £449 (required repair).    Optional repairs included:  * Keyboard replacement: £329  * Screen replacement: £549  With multi-repair discounts applied:  * Logic board + keyboard: £678 inc VAT  * Logic board + screen: £848 inc VAT  * Full repair (logic board, keyboard, screen): £1,027 inc VAT  The customer accepted to proceed with the £1,027 repair.  Reassembly:  As the customer was unable to proceed with payment, the repair was not completed. The previously repaired CPU data line was reversed, and the device was reassembled back to its original non-working state, ready for collection.    Current request:  The client requested to proceed with the £1,027 repair. I advised that we need to carry out another chargeable diagnostic and that should the diagnostic report match, we would waive it off the final cost of repair.

We have completed our diagnostic on your MacBook Pro 14" (M1 Pro, 2021) and identified the following fault:  Trackpad Flex Cable: Our diagnostic confirmed an issue with the trackpad flex cable. This is the cable that links both the trackpad and the keyboard to the logic board, so when it fails, neither of them can communicate with the device. That's why both the trackpad and keyboard have stopped responding.  We checked the device thoroughly and didn't find any signs of liquid damage, and there isn't a clear cause for the failure. ------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic Board: Our diagnostic confirmed a localised explosion on the main charging circuit of the device. This was likely caused by a power surge or a faulty charger that overstressed a specific microchip, which then dragged the entire circuit down with it. We can resolve this by repairing the charging circuit. * Battery: The logic board issue then spread through the charging circuit to the rest of the device and eventually reached the battery, which has also been damaged. As a result, the BMS (battery management system, the built-in controller board) has failed, meaning the battery can no longer communicate with the device. We can resolve this by replacing it. * Charging Ports (optional): Our diagnostic confirmed that all four charging ports are loose due to wear and tear. They are still working, but may worsen over time until they are no longer able to securely latch onto cables. The order was placed under the name Hasan Ishola, using the email address hassanis9302@gmail.com and phone number +44 7803 179093. Payment was made using a Mastercard ending in 9526. The billing address provided was 1 Cranbrook Road, Thornton Heath, CR7 8PQ.  The individual attended our premises in person on 9 April 2026 at approximately 12:30 PM for a walk-in appointment and dropped off a MacBook Pro for repair. The person who attended on this date was a Black male, approximately 25 years old. He also communicated with us via email over the following days regarding the device’s faults.  On 10 April 2026, we received an email from Giulia Sasso (giuliasasso32@gmail.com), a resident of Switzerland, informing us that £179 had been charged to her card without her knowledge or authorisation at 00:17 on 10 April (Swiss time), which corresponds to the time of the Shopify transaction on 8 April at 11:17 PM UK time. She confirmed the last four digits of her card were 9526, matching the card used for the order. She stated that nobody else had access to her card and that her payment details had been stolen from a fraudulent car rental website she had used.  We immediately placed the order on hold, ceased all work on the device, and contacted Hasan Ishola to investigate. We also advised Giulia Sasso to report the matter to her bank and have the card blocked, which she confirmed she had done. The payment of £179.00 (£173.92 net after Shopify processing) was added to our payout on 13 April 2026 and the order was marked as fulfilled before the fraud was reported to us.  The device was collected on 13 April. The individual who collected the device was a Black female, also approximately 25 years old, and was not the same person who had originally dropped off the device. Faults Found ------------  * Logic Board: Our diagnostic confirmed liquid damage across multiple sections of the logic board, primarily around the CD (charging controller) ICs, which manage accessory detection and power negotiation, as well as the main charging circuit itself. The affected lines can be restored by rebuilding the damaged areas. We also noted missing screws and signs of a previous cleaning attempt on the board. * Lid Angle Sensor: The hall (lid angle) sensor has also been affected by liquid damage and requires repair to ensure proper sleep/wake functionality. We will clean the corrosion and rebuild the damaged circuitry, as replacing the sensor assembly would disable automatic sleep/wake behaviour. * Battery: The installed battery is a third-party component, which may impact long-term performance and calibration. * Keyboard: Some keys are sticky due to residual liquid under the key mechanisms. The keyboard is functional, but this may affect responsiveness over time. * Speakers: The left speaker is distorted from liquid ingress and should be replaced to restore full audio performance. The right speaker remains functional.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, please see your options below. Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Liquid has corroded several power and signal points on the main board. Although the MacBook still takes charge, it cannot power up correctly due to the several failures on board. * Display: The original screen shows no image because its internal T‑CON (timing‑controller) board was shorted by liquid. This will need a full display replacement. * Keyboard: Liquid seeped through the keys and damaged the keyboard’s internal circuit, so key presses are no longer recognised. A full keyboard replacement is required. * Trackpad: The same spill reached the trackpad; its flex cable and touch circuitry are corroded, so the cursor and click no longer register. * Keyboard back‑light board (keyboard logic board): This small board distributes power from the logic board to the keyboard. Corrosion here prevents the new keyboard from working unless this board is replaced as well. * Lid Angle Sensor: The magnetic sensor that tells the MacBook when the lid is open or closed was damaged by the spill. Replacing it restores normal sleep‑wake behaviour.  Due to the extent of the damage, completing a full repair would not be economically viable. Instead, we can proceed with a data recovery, which will allow you to retrieve the information stored on the device and transfer it to a new one. We can either complete an iCloud backup, copy the data to an external SSD, or transfer it directly onto your replacement MacBook.  Repair Quote ------------  * MacBook Tier 2 Data Recovery: £399 Total: £399 inc VAT  Once approved, the data recovery will be completed within 2 working days.

Issues Found ------------  * Logic board: Our initial diagnostics confirmed that the iPhone powers on, but neither Wi‑Fi nor the SIM card were being recognised. We managed to restore Wi‑Fi by repairing the top layer of the logic board, which tells us that part is working correctly. However, the SIM is still not being detected, which means there is damage on the lower layer of the board - the part responsible for mobile signal and SIM communication. Because that section cannot be repaired on its own, we would need to replace the entire bottom layer of the logic board to restore full connectivity.  We can confirm that your iPhone is repairable, please see your options below. records.item_name: Cristopher Hamilton *Will get back to us at the end of the month
records.text: Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Liquid reached the Mac’s central power spine, short-circuiting the high-current rails that supply the M1 Pro chip, the USB-C charge controller, and the display-power IC. The surge damaged dozens of components, including capacitors, sense resistors, and backlight drivers, and caused torn copper and scorched pads across two internal board layers. To restore the board, we will need to strip and ultrasonic-clean it, rebuild missing tracks, and microsolder new power-management chips and capacitors across five zones. Without this repair, the board would remain stuck at 5V and never hand over power to the CPU, effectively bricking the device. This repair is essential to bring the Mac back to life and preserve your data. * Screen: The spill also damaged the LED chain that powers the lower-right backlight zone, leaving that corner visibly dim. A screen replacement is required to restore full and even brightness. This repair is optional. * Keyboard: Light corrosion is visible on the keyboard’s switch matrix. Although all keys are currently working, oxidation may continue to spread and cause intermittent key failures over time. A keyboard replacement is advised if you would prefer to avoid that risk. This repair is optional.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, please see your options below.  Required Repairs ----------------  * MacBook Pro 16 M1 Pro/Max A2485 Tier 4 Logic Board Repair: £799 Total: £ inc VAT  Optional Repairs ----------------  * MacBook Pro 16 M1 Pro/Max A2485 Keyboard: £289 * MacBook Pro 16 M1 Pro/Max A2485 Screen: £549 Total (including only keyboard): £1,088 inc VAT  Total (including only screen): £1,348 inc VAT  Total (including both screen and keyboard): £1,637 inc VAT  Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 3 working days once approved.
